topic: Southern Distance Quadratic — post-queue_140 fix-pass review
change_summary: Queue pushed 2026-04-15T19:42 citing arithmetic fix (520->52), TLDR V50.4/V50.6 framing, and Vincenty description. All three c3 hole fixes verified in current text. detail_evidence 5142->5268 chars (+126), detail_verdict unchanged (952), tldr_evidence 345->456, tldr_verdict 132->317.
our_argument_summary: The queued fix-pass successfully lands the three largest c3 holes: the 520 m/km arithmetic error is now 52 m/km; the TLDR now leads with V50.4-vs-V50.6 framing so a casual reader sees the supersession; the Vincenty description cites Karney 2013 with absolute-error framing. Verdict Misleading holds. Remaining defects are smaller but should not be left to compound.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No change since c3: find_curve.py still a skeleton with 6 hardcoded cities and an empty body; no repo script performs the claimed 20-route regression. Quadratic coefficients and R²=0.7874 are static strings. V50.6 H(r) parameters H₀=8537, λ=8619 also static.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded throughout. The 5.2% mean and 0.0% Oslo–Stockholm headline are static display strings; nothing re-derives them from raw route data.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The retreat-to-V50.6 escape is closed by the text (both stages evaluated and both shown equally post-hoc). No new escape clause opens.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None detected. c3 structural claims (find_curve.py skeleton, no 20-route regression, post-hoc evolution note) remain accurate against raw-text.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-027. The quadratic coefficients, R²=0.7874, and the V50.6 5.2%/0.0% numbers are all static in raw-text/02-wins.txt and the rendered wins.html — no fetch, no regression.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The quadratic distance distortion is the signature of the azimuthal-equidistant projection from sphere to plane. Standard cartography predicts exactly this shape; the dome labels it a law.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Raw-text/02-wins.txt evolution note: v22 circular-disc failure on southern/cross-equator distances was patched by WIN-027 (quadratic) and WIN-030 (ellipse). The quadratic was invented AFTER the failure, not predicted before it.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc_radius or firmament_height to the coefficients 0.00131, 0.06828, 1.06719. The values are regression outputs; no script performs the regression on an enumerated 20-route set.

holes_found.description: Parameter-list factual error inside WIN-027. The text states the V50.6 distance mechanism uses 'two fitted parameters (amplitude 8,537 and scale length 8,619), plus an east–west angular scale factor of 0.9941, plus an ellipse axis ratio (WIN-030) — four free parameters tuned against 19 city pairs.' The ellipse axis ratio (WIN-030) is a fit for the astronomical tropic-ellipse shape — it does not appear in the V50.6 distance computation at all. The V13 two-zone topology's fourth distance parameter is the equatorial ring radius r_eq = 14,105 km (as correctly stated in WIN-057). Listing the ellipse axis ratio as a distance parameter is a category error that a technically competent defender can exploit: 'The critics claim our distance formula uses the ellipse axis ratio — it doesn't. They haven't read our model.' This is also the root cause of the cross-WIN inconsistency c3 flagged (WIN-027 vs WIN-057 different fourth parameter).
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'plus an ellipse axis ratio (WIN-030)' with 'plus the equatorial ring radius r_eq = 14,105 km introduced in V13 two-zone topology' so WIN-027 and WIN-057 enumerate the same four distance parameters (H₀, λ, EW scale 0.9941, r_eq). If the argument intends to count cumulative geometric parameters across WIN-027/WIN-030/WIN-057, state that explicitly as 'five to six parameters across the distance-and-shape stack' rather than rolling one into the distance formula where it doesn't belong.

holes_found.description: Geometric sloppiness in the Sydney–Santiago kill-shot: 'Sydney sits on the ~20,015 km southern rim and Santiago on the opposing rim.' The flat-disc AE projection has only one outer rim (the disc's perimeter at r ≈ 20,015 km) — there is no 'opposing rim.' Under linear colat mapping with r_eq = 14,105 km, Sydney (~34°S) and Santiago (~33.5°S) sit at r ≈ 16,300 km each, on nearly opposite azimuths (~138° apart) of the same southern ring. The chord length still exceeds ~30,000 km (verified in c3), but a defender who knows the geometry can respond: 'The critics say we have two rims; we have one. They don't even know where our cities are.' The rhetorical damage is disproportionate to the substantive error.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the geometric setup: 'On the flat disc, Sydney (~34°S) and Santiago (~33.5°S) sit near the southern edge at r ≈ 16,000 km from centre, separated by ~138° of azimuth; the planar chord between them exceeds ~30,000 km, almost three times the globe great-circle distance.' This keeps the kill-shot but ditches the incorrect 'opposing rims' framing.

holes_found.description: The Karney/Vincenty sentence now has doubled phrasing: 'achieves sub-millimeter absolute accuracy on WGS84 (sub-millimetre absolute error on the full WGS84 ellipsoid, per Karney 2013 — accuracy does not degrade with distance)'. The parenthetical restates the main clause, and 'sub-millimeter' (American) and 'sub-millimetre' (British) sit back-to-back. The c3 recommendation was to REPLACE the old per-km framing with the Karney absolute-error framing; the decider appended the new inside the old, creating redundancy. A defender won't attack on grammar, but it reads as mechanical and weakens the line's authority.
holes_found.recommendation: Consolidate to one clause: 'the globe's Vincenty (1975) algorithm, refined by Karney (2013), delivers sub-millimetre absolute accuracy on the WGS84 ellipsoid — error does not accumulate with distance — using exactly two parameters (semi-major axis 6,378,137 m and flattening 1/298.257223563) and zero latitude-dependent corrections anywhere on Earth.'

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN parameter-count inconsistency with WIN-057 remains unresolved. WIN-027 says the four V50.6 parameters are (H₀, λ, EW scale, ellipse axis ratio). WIN-057 says (H₀, λ, EW scale, equatorial ring radius). Both can't be right. Per hole #1, the WIN-057 list is the correct distance-computation list; WIN-027's enumeration is the one that needs fixing. This is a consistency defect first (two of our pages say different things) and a correctness defect second.
holes_found.recommendation: Fix hole #1 and this hole resolves automatically. Both pages should cite: (H₀ = 8,537 km, λ = 8,619 km, EW angular scale 0.9941, equatorial ring radius r_eq = 14,105 km). Optionally note that disc_radius = 20,015 km is also present but is not directly fit to distance residuals.

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text still opens with 'R-sq 0.79 = 21% unexplained; globe great-circle achieves <0.5% error' framing (from the summary-table finding). In the detail_evidence we now cite sub-millimetre absolute accuracy on WGS84 — essentially zero error. The <0.5% bound in the verdict text and the 'achieves <0.5% error' in the summary finding drastically under-sell Vincenty/Karney accuracy vs the dome's 5.2%. A dome defender can't use this against us (it's only a weaker-than-necessary bound), but it makes our argument rhetorically flatter than it should be.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the finding to 'R² 0.79 = 21% unexplained; spherical geodesy achieves sub-millimetre absolute accuracy globally with two parameters.' Trim the matching clause from detail_verdict_text or drop it in favour of the already-present detail_evidence formulation.

holes_found.description: The generic post-hoc boilerplate paragraph in detail_verdict_text ('Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test...') is redundant here. The detail_evidence already names the exact post-hoc mechanism (v22 failure → quadratic patch → 20-route regression claim unreproduced in repo). Two voices saying the same thing in different registers reads as boilerplate. Repeated from c3; low priority but worth noting the decider did not act on it.
holes_found.recommendation: Either trim the boilerplate to one anchor-link sentence ([full post-hoc standard in Part 3.1b]) or drop it entirely for WINs whose detail_evidence already makes the post-hoc case explicit.

holes_found.description: The QF27/QF28 'nonstop' framing flagged in c3 is still present. Depending on the current schedule, QF27/QF28 sometimes operates via Auckland. The kill-shot argument is unaffected by stop structure (30,000 km disc chord can't be flown in ~13 h of air time whether with or without an AKL stop), but the brittle nonstop claim is an easy minor attack surface.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to 'Direct Sydney–Santiago services (operated nonstop by Qantas at various points, and via Auckland at others) complete ~11,338 km in roughly 13 h of air time — a disc route nearly three times that distance cannot be flown in that window at subsonic cruise speed regardless of stop structure.'

stronger_arguments.description: The V50.6 coordinate conversion r(city) solves r·tan(polaris_elevation) = H(r). Polaris elevation equals latitude on a sphere — so the input to the dome's 'disc' distance formula is itself a globe-derived coordinate. WIN-027 gestures at this ('takes a globe-derived input') but does not name Polaris-elevation = latitude as the parasitism link. The strongest form is: 'The dome cannot get from a city to its r(city) without latitude; it cannot get latitude without the round Earth; therefore every V50.6 distance is conditional on globe coordinates.' This mirrors the WGS-84 dependency argument in WIN-058 and would close an explicit loop across the distance stack.

The Oslo–Stockholm 0.0% result is algebraically forced, not rhetorically cherry-picked. Both cities sit near latitude 59.3–59.9°N (Δlat < 1°). At near-equal latitudes, both disc and sphere distance formulas reduce to the same shape in Δθ once the EW angular scale factor is fit to the distance at that latitude. The dome's 0.9941 scale is itself tuned on a near-identical-latitude pair; therefore the test-set residual at any near-identical-latitude pair is trivially ~0. 0.0% Oslo–Stockholm is a tautology of the fit, not evidence of prediction.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that projecting a round Earth onto a flat disc produces systematic, latitude-dependent distance distortions, and the approximate quadratic shape of those distortions at mid-to-southern latitudes is a real mathematical pattern (the AE projection signature). Recognizing the pattern is legitimate cartography; fitting a correction to it is legitimate curve-fitting.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The distortion the dome fits IS the fingerprint of spherical curvature forced onto a plane. A quadratic correction to planar distance is the diagnostic of sphere-to-plane projection. Claiming this signature as a dome prediction means the dome has rediscovered the curvature it denies and labelled that rediscovery a win. The kernel of truth proves the opposite of what the dome claims.

deeper_analysis:
The queue-140 fix-pass cleanly lands the three biggest c3 holes. '52 m per km' is now correct. The TLDR explicitly names V50.4 and V50.6 so a casual reader sees the supersession. Karney 2013 is cited with absolute-error language.

What remains is second-order. The most substantive remaining defect (hole #1) is the ellipse-axis-ratio misidentification as a distance parameter — a dome defender who knows the V13 two-zone topology can say 'you listed the wrong fourth parameter' and peel back one layer of the critique. This is both a WIN-027 factual error and the mechanical cause of the cross-WIN inconsistency with WIN-057. Fixing it in WIN-027 fixes two holes at once.

The Sydney–Santiago 'opposing rims' framing (hole #2) is a rhetorical mis-step rather than a substantive error — the chord arithmetic still stands — but it hands the defender a 'critics don't know our geometry' line. Both moderate holes are fixable with targeted text edits; no structural rewrite needed.

The Karney doubling (hole #3) and the cross-WIN consistency (hole #4) are consequences of the decider's patch style in this pass — inserting new language next to old language rather than replacing. Worth a light polish pass.

Verdict 'Misleading' holds. Confidence 0.90 (up from c3 0.88): the three biggest c3 holes are gone, and the remaining holes are smaller and mostly textual.

citations_verified: Disc chord Sydney–Santiago ~30,000 km — verified c3 (linear colat mapping gives 30,476 km)
5.2% of 1 km = 52 m — arithmetic now correct in text
citations_failed: 'plus an ellipse axis ratio (WIN-030)' as the fourth V50.6 distance parameter — factually wrong; should be equatorial ring radius 14,105 km (see WIN-057)
'Sydney sits on the ~20,015 km southern rim and Santiago on the opposing rim' — flat disc has one rim; both cities sit at r ≈ 16,000 km on the same southern ring, separated by ~138° of azimuth
citations_unchecked: QF27/QF28 current operating configuration (nonstop vs via AKL) — schedule-dependent; kill-shot holds either way

advocate_mode.best_defense: A prepared defender would attack three ways. (1) 'You listed the ellipse axis ratio as one of our four distance parameters. It isn't in the distance formula — WIN-030 is about the astronomical tropic ellipse. You haven't understood what you're criticizing.' (2) 'You claim Sydney sits on one rim and Santiago on the opposing rim. Our disc has one rim — the outer edge. Both cities are in the southern interior. You don't know our geometry.' (3) 'Your own summary finding says spherical geodesy achieves <0.5% error, but your detail paragraph says sub-millimetre — which is it? Pick a bound and stick with it.' None of these touch the verdict, but all three let the defender claim 'the critics are sloppy; their substantive claims can't be trusted.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Hole #1 (ellipse-vs-r_eq) and hole #2 (opposing rims vs single rim) are the two prongs worth closing immediately. Both are short text edits, both are fully within the detail_evidence paragraph that was just patched. Once those land, the remaining attack surface is cosmetic (hole #3 Karney doubling, hole #5 finding bound tightening) and a defender has to work much harder to generate a quality-control narrative. Target defense_survives after the fixes: 1–2.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-027 and WIN-057 still enumerate the V50.6 distance parameters differently. WIN-027 says (H₀, λ, EW scale, ellipse axis ratio). WIN-057 says (H₀, λ, EW scale, equatorial ring radius 14,105 km). The WIN-057 enumeration matches the V13 two-zone topology described in raw-text/05-model.txt; the WIN-027 enumeration incorrectly promotes WIN-030's ellipse ratio into the distance formula. Fixing WIN-027's list to match WIN-057 resolves both the cross-WIN inconsistency (c3 hole #3) and the standalone factual error (c4 hole #1). Separately, WIN-027 still does not cross-link to WIN-058's WGS-84 dependency argument, which would strengthen the cumulative parasitism case.

claims_checked: 5.2% × 1 km = 52 m — text now correct (c3 arithmetic error fixed)
Sydney–Santiago great-circle 11,338 km — verified (haversine 11,346 km, within rounding)
Disc chord ~30,000 km with r_eq=14,105, disc_radius=20,015, linear colat mapping, Δθ=138.12° — verified 30,476 km (c3 independent check)
Adjusted R² ≈ 0.75 with n=20, p=3, R²=0.7874 — verified 0.747
Vincenty/Karney sub-millimetre absolute accuracy on WGS84 — consistent with Karney 2013
quantitative_verification.computations: Spot-checked the newly-introduced text: 5.2% of 1 km = 52 m confirmed; 5.2% of 10,000 km = 520 km confirmed (both now consistent in the parenthetical and the trans-hemispheric statement). Parameter-count claim (four) reviewed against WIN-057 and raw-text — hole #1 identifies the specific mis-named parameter.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: One factual error remains: 'plus an ellipse axis ratio (WIN-030)' as the fourth V50.6 distance parameter. The correct fourth parameter per WIN-057 and raw-text/05-model.txt is the equatorial ring radius r_eq = 14,105 km.
